title: Group-DRO over season-regime environments instead of average-risk training
summary: Robust-optimization papers show ERM can overfit majority environments and underperform on atypical groups.
rationale: Robust-optimization papers show ERM can overfit majority environments and underperform on atypical groups. This dataset mixes years, races, wet/dry laps, and rare compounds, so worst-group robustness may matter more than another average-case feature tweak.
implementation_hint: Define soft or hard environments such as Year x wet/dry x race phase x tyre-age bin, or Race x Compound. Train a small neural/tabular expert, or reweight tree-training rows, with a DRO-style objective that upweights high-loss groups. Select by worst-group AUC and forward-fold robustness, not overall CV alone.
expected_effect: May reduce leaderboard fragility by protecting against rare but important slices like wet/intermediate laps or shifted 2025-style race patterns.
risk: Average CV can stay flat or drop slightly while public LB improves; environment design is critical and too many tiny groups will make training noisy.
